bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My top values across all issues are: issueA=valueF (1.0), issueB=valueF (1.0), issueC=valueF (1.0), issueD=valueF (1.0). This gives me maximum utility of 1.0. Early in the negotiation, I should anchor high.

It's very early (t=0.02), so I can stay close to my top-utility bid. The opponent opened with their best bid (all valueA, which is their maximum but my minimum utility), and their fake urgency message is cheap talk designed to pressure me into conceding early. I'll make a small concession from my opening max-utility bid on lower-weight issues (issueA and issueB) while holding firm on issueC and issueD which have weight 0.3 each.

The opponent is clearly using fake urgency tactics ("window closing", "act now") at t=0.04 which is extremely early in the negotiation. I should not be pressured by their false urgency claims. My previous bid was already a reasonable concession from my opening (dropping issueA and issueB from valueF to valueE). I'll hold near my high-utility positions since we're only at 4% through the negotiation. I'll make a slight concession on issueA (low importance at 0.1) but hold firm on issueC and issueD (both 0.3 importance).
